grant_title: Understanding, Remediating, and Reducing Per- and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances (PFAS) Contamination Related to Food Systems
project_area: PFAS contamination remediation and mitigation in Maine's food system, including agricultural settings, wild-caught foods, detection methods, and remediation technologies
demographic_requirements: Projects must engage with stakeholders such as farmers, community groups, and regulatory agencies in Maine. Priority given to projects that support PFAS-impacted farmers and citizens.
eligibility_summary: Open to research institutions, non-profits, government agencies, tribal organizations, and academic institutions. Projects must focus on PFAS contamination in Maine's food system and align with research needs proposed by the Maine Department of Agriculture, Conservation, and Forestry (DACF) PFAS Fund. Grants range from $50,000 to $350,000.
